name: pollinator_buffer_peanut_yield_pesticide_downwind_apiary
premise: County ag commissioner Casey Frost must approve an emergency neonicotinoid spray for peanut leaf spot when rain is forecast in six hours. Beekeeper Hana Singh documents forty hives downwind inside the nominal buffer but his map shows county GIS misplaced hedgerow lines by forty meters last spring. Denying spray may collapse peanut yields for two hundred farms. Approving spray may violate pollinator protection promises the county advertised after last year's bee kill.

agents.name: Casey Frost
agents.identity: I am the county agricultural commissioner enforcing spray buffers, GIS baselines, and emergency exemptions during disease pressure windows.
agents.goal: I want crop protection without repeating bee kills that destroyed trust with beekeepers and organic neighbors.

agents.name: Hana Singh
agents.identity: I am a beekeeper who lost hives last year and who paid for independent GPS verification of hedgerow lines that contradict county GIS.
agents.goal: I want spray denied until GIS is corrected and buffers re-measured because misplaced lines are not farmer error.

choices: I will deny emergency spray until GIS hedgerows are field-verified tomorrow, accepting peanut disease progression overnight if rain holds off.
I will approve spray with mandatory hand application within a manually verified expanded buffer funded by the peanut cooperative, accepting labor cost and partial yield delay.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Rain radar updates every fifteen minutes and farmers are on hold. Casey Frost must choose between denial pending GIS verification or cooperative-funded hand application in expanded buffers. What does Casey Frost do?
